recommendations.recommendation: Embedding is the current largest timed bottleneck. Reuse cached embeddings when training/reranking only, and keep multi-GPU sentence-transformer encoding enabled for larger LeanRank-data refreshes.

Proof-state Recall@100 is low, so proof-state premise retrieval is currently limited by candidate generation or embeddings before reranking. Prioritize stronger proof-state/query representations, domain-aware candidate pools, and embedding model comparisons before adding heavier rerankers.
`sample` is the largest CPU/IO-heavy stage at 27.5904 seconds per 100k processed rows. Use this profile to prioritize CPU/vectorization work before the next larger LeanRank-data refresh.

config.query_prefix: Represent this Lean proof state for retrieving useful premises:

test_set_evaluation.label_policy: held-out test positive_edges are used only for evaluation

embedding.query_prefix: Represent this Lean proof state for retrieving useful premises:

example_traces.goal_text: hf'.lift' i h hi ≫ hf'.snd g = h
(ofCocone c).map (homOfLE ⋯) = (ofCoconeObjIso c i hi).hom ≫ c.ι.app ⟨i, hi⟩ ≫ (ofCoconeObjIsoPt c).inv

trace_profile.negative_candidate_hardness_source: computed from normalized positive and negative premise namespace/domain overlap
trace_profile.positive_trace_source: LeanRank-data pos_premise and all_pos_premises normalized to positive_edges

notes: Storage projection scales current artifact bytes linearly with processed row count.
Index artifacts dominate current storage and should be monitored when scaling LeanRank-data runs.
Unreferenced index artifacts are files under outputs/indexes that are not referenced by the current index manifests.

cpu_io_efficiency_profile.interpretation: CPU/IO stage costs are normalized by processed split rows so future LeanRank-data runs can compare sampling, normalization, graph augmentation, validation, and difficulty computation without rerunning embeddings.

execution_mode_summary.bottleneck_interpretation: embedding is still the largest timed stage even with GPU encoding, so artifact reuse matters for report and reranking refreshes
execution_mode_summary.ranker_retrain_policy: Do not retrain by default. Reuse embeddings, indexes, and trained models for report/homepage refreshes; rerun ranker training only after ranker feature, label, split, or relevant config changes.

metric_uncertainty_profile.note: Intervals are approximate diagnostics for bounded aggregate retrieval metrics, not a replacement for paired bootstrap comparisons.

gates.evidence: Production performance profile must use the configured LeanRank-data source.
gates.name: target_dataset
gates.threshold: erbacher/LeanRank-data

gates.evidence: Performance numbers should describe a non-demo LeanRank-data run.
gates.name: large_scale_slice
gates.threshold: >=60000 processed split rows and scale_bucket=large

gates.evidence: Final retrieval claims should use full held-out proof-state and theorem evaluation.
gates.name: full_heldout_evaluation
gates.threshold: both coverage fractions == 1.0

gates.evidence: Bottleneck shares and throughput estimates must come from a non-cached production timing run.
gates.name: fresh_pipeline_timing
gates.threshold: scale_estimate_reliable=true and throughput_basis=executed_pipeline_run

gates.evidence: Large-scale retrieval should use indexed candidate generation with measurable speedup.
gates.name: ann_speedup
gates.threshold: >=5x mean indexed speedup vs exact cosine

gates.evidence: ANN speedup should not substantially change nearest-neighbor candidate quality.
gates.name: ann_recall
gates.threshold: >=0.95 minimum Recall@10 vs exact cosine across indexed entities

gates.evidence: Embedding remains the primary bottleneck, so GPU encoding should stay enabled for production refreshes.
gates.name: gpu_embedding_parallelism
gates.threshold: cuda requested with at least one device

gates.evidence: Held-out ranking evaluation should use the batched GPU path when available.
gates.name: gpu_evaluation_backend
gates.threshold: actual_backends includes torch_cuda

gates.evidence: Report and homepage refreshes should reuse embeddings, indexes, and trained models unless inputs change.
gates.name: artifact_reuse_ready
gates.threshold: reuse_by_default=true

gates.evidence: The report should expose enough throughput data to compare future embedding optimizations.
gates.name: embedding_throughput_recorded
gates.threshold: >0 embedding rows/sec

stability_profile.interpretation: Use validation/test agreement and improvement over stored_embedding before changing the production proof_state_query_representation.

recommended_sequence.reason: Proof-state gold premises are often absent from the top-100 candidate pool, so top-k reranking cannot recover them. Validation query diagnostic currently favors `stored_plus_goal_only`.
Theorem-level Recall@100 is substantially higher than Recall@10, leaving useful headroom for ordering candidates already in the pool.
Ranker ablation says `frequency` is the strongest currently measured feature group by delta_without_group.
LeanRank-data already provides positive premises and hard negative candidates, so training/evaluation changes can reuse existing labels without extracting new data.

scenarios.reason: Presentation-only changes can reuse the committed LeanRank-data artifacts and regenerate reports/homepage.
Ranking logic changes require held-out metrics, but embeddings and indexes remain reusable when query/embedding configuration and data splits are unchanged.
Feature or supervision changes invalidate the learned ranker; they do not require re-embedding unless the embedding text/model changed.
Embedding model, prefix, device-independent representation text, or vector dimensions invalidate embeddings, indexes, and learned scores.
Changing the LeanRank-data sample or theorem-disjoint split changes rows, labels, negatives, graph edges, embeddings, indexes, and test metrics.

refresh_reuse_profile.training_repeat_policy: Do not retrain by default. Reuse embeddings, indexes, and trained models for report/homepage refreshes; rerun ranker training only after ranker feature, label, split, or relevant config changes.

rerank_evaluation_cost_profile.policy: keep reranked proof-state evaluation sampled for development and use full batched embedding evaluation for final held-out coverage

assumptions: Projection scales current timed pipeline seconds linearly with processed row count.
Embedding and index-build estimates scale their timed stage seconds linearly.
This is for capacity planning and should be replaced by a fresh timing run after changing hardware, embedding model, index backend, or sample shape.

gates.evidence: Premise ranking needs proof-state-to-premise positives from LeanRank-data.
gates.name: positive_edges_present
gates.threshold: >0 normalized positive premise edges

gates.evidence: Ranker and difficulty experiments need negative candidates, not only positives.
gates.name: negative_candidates_present
gates.threshold: >0 normalized hard-negative candidate edges

gates.evidence: The same proof-state/premise pair must not be both a positive and negative label.
gates.name: positive_negative_pairs_disjoint
gates.threshold: true after normalization conflict removal

gates.evidence: KG supervision edges must resolve to existing proof-state and premise nodes.
gates.name: edge_endpoints_valid
gates.threshold: positive and negative edge endpoints valid

gates.evidence: Training split should cover nearly all proof states with positive premise supervision.
gates.name: train_positive_coverage
gates.threshold: >=0.95 train proof-state positive coverage

gates.evidence: Training split should cover nearly all proof states with hard-negative candidates.
gates.name: train_negative_coverage
gates.threshold: >=0.95 train proof-state negative candidate coverage

gates.evidence: Hard-negative diagnostics should compare negatives to positives from the same proof state.
gates.name: hard_negative_pair_evidence
gates.threshold: >0 train negative/positive comparison pairs

gates.evidence: Difficulty and hard-negative reports need a usable negative-candidate hardness signal.
gates.name: hardness_feature_present
gates.threshold: negative_candidate_hardness exists
